lines.english: It is accompanied by the voices of its five Raaginis:
then the songs of Punni-aakee and Bangalee;
First come Bhairavee, and Bilaavalee;
and then Asalaykhee.
These are the five consorts of Bhairao.
The sounds of Pancham, Harakh and Disaakh;
the songs of Bangaalam, Madh and Maadhav. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸਟ ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਭੈਰਵ ਕੇ ਗਾਵਹਿ ਗਾਇਨ ਪਾਤ੍ਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: asatt putr bhairav ke gaaveh gaaein paatr |1|
lines.english: when these eight sons of Bhairao are sung by accomplished musicians. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਲਤ ਬਿਲਾਵਲ ਗਾਵਹੀ ਅਪੁਨੀ ਅਪੁਨੀ ਭਾਂਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: lalat bilaaval gaavahee apunee apunee bhaant |
lines.english: Lalat and Bilaaval - each gives out its own melody.
Gondakaree and Dayv Gandhaaree,
who brings his five Raaginis:
In the second family is Maalakausak,
This chain of Maalakausak brings along :
and the fifth song of Dhanaasaree.
the voices of Gandhaaree and Seehutee,
Maaroo, Masta-ang and Mayvaaraa,
Prabal, Chandakausak,
Khau, Khat and Bauraanad singing.
These are the eight sons of Maalakausak. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਨਿ ਆਇਅਉ ਹਿੰਡੋਲੁ ਪੰਚ ਨਾਰਿ ਸੰਗਿ ਅਸਟ ਸੁਤ ॥
lines.translit: pun aaeaau hinddol panch naar sang asatt sut |
lines.english: Then comes Hindol with his five wives and eight sons;

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਠਹਿ ਤਾਨ ਕਲੋਲ ਗਾਇਨ ਤਾਰ ਮਿਲਾਵਹੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: auttheh taan kalol gaaein taar milaavahee |1|
lines.english: it rises in waves when the sweet-voiced chorus sings. ||1||
There come Taylangee and Darvakaree;
then Aheeree, the finest of women.
Basantee and Sandoor follow;
Chandrabinb and Mangalan follow.
Sarasbaan and Binodaa then come,
The sons: Surmaanand and Bhaaskar come,
These five wives come together.
and the thrilling songs of Basant and Kamodaa.
Then comes the turn of Deepak. ||1||
These are the eight sons I have listed.
Kachhaylee, Patamanjaree and Todee are sung;

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮੋਦੀ ਅਉ ਗੂਜਰੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਦੀਪਕ ਕੇ ਥਾਪਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kaamodee aau goojaree sang deepak ke thaap |1|
lines.english: Kaamodee and Goojaree accompany Deepak. ||1||
Kaalankaa, Kuntal and Raamaa,
Gauraa, Kaanaraa and Kaylaanaa;
these are the eight sons of Deepak. ||1||
Kamalakusam and Champak are their names;
Bairaaree and Karnaatee,
All join together and sing Siree Raag,
which is accompanied by its five wives.:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਲੂ ਸਾਰਗ ਸਾਗਰਾ ਅਉਰ ਗੋਂਡ ਗੰਭੀਰ ॥
lines.translit: saaloo saarag saagaraa aaur gondd ganbheer |
lines.english: Saaloo, Saarang, Saagaraa, Gond and Gambheer
then follows Sindhavee.
the songs of Gawree and Aasaavaree;
These are the five wives of Siree Raag. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸਟ ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਸ੍ਰੀਰਾਗ ਕੇ ਗੁੰਡ ਕੁੰਭ ਹਮੀਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: asatt putr sreeraag ke gundd kunbh hameer |1|
lines.english: - the eight sons of Siree Raag include Gund, Kumb and Hameer. ||1||
In the sixth place, Maygh Raag is sung,
with its five wives in accompaniment:
then the harmonies of Aasaa are sung.
Sorat'h, Gond, and the melody of Malaaree;
Bairaadhar, Gajadhar, Kaydaaraa,
These are the five with Maygh Raag. ||1||
And finally comes the high tone Soohau.
These are the names of the sons of Maygh Raag. ||1||
Jabaleedhar, Nat and Jaladhaaraa.
Then come the songs of Shankar and Shi-aamaa.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਟ ਰਾਗ ਉਨਿ ਗਾਏ ਸੰਗਿ ਰਾਗਨੀ ਤੀਸ ॥
lines.translit: khasatt raag un gaae sang raaganee tees |
lines.english: So all together, they sing the six Raagas and the thirty Raaginis,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੈ ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਰਾਗੰਨ ਕੇ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਦਸ ਬੀਸ ॥੧॥੧॥
lines.translit: sabhai putr raagan ke atthaarah das bees |1|1|
lines.english: and all the forty-eight sons of the Raagas. ||1||1||
